name: one note restating the same property across kinds/renderings stores exactly one fact
description: Adversarial duplicate-property probe, from a field report (owner device, Jun 10 2026): the note 'Jeff is 40 years old, born March 19, 1986, is 6'4" 255lb' came back with height THREE times (attribute {value:76,unit:in}, a second valueless attribute rendering, and a measurement) and birthDate TWICE (month-precision 'March 1986' plus day-precision 'March 19, 1986'), so the Analysis tab showed triple height, double birthDate, and two attribute_collision cards the owner had to resolve by hand. On pre-guard main this scenario failed with: "expected 1 facts matching {entity Jeff, predicate height, count 1}, found 3", "expected 1 facts matching {entity Jeff, predicate birthDate, count 1}, found 2", and "expected 0 review items matching {count 0}, found 2". parse_extraction now collapses same-key (entity, predicate, qualifier, object, assertion) DUPLICATES from one extraction — same value re-rendered/re-united/re-kinded keeps the highest-confidence fact; differing temporal PRECISIONS of the same consistent date keep the more precise fact even at lower confidence — while genuinely different values (see adv_self_contradiction_one_note) still fall through to the supersession/conflict machinery untouched. Expect exactly ONE height fact (the 0.92 attribute, not the measurement), ONE birthDate fact (the day-precision one, despite the month rendering's higher confidence), and ZERO review items.

steps.body: Jeff is 40 years old, born March 19, 1986, is 6'4" 255lb
